# CircleCI CLI

Manage MCP servers for AI assistants and code editors

## Usage

`circleci mcp`

## Available Commands

| Command  | Description                       |
| -------- | --------------------------------- |
| `claude` | Manage Claude Desktop MCP servers |
| `cursor` | Manage Cursor MCP servers         |
| `start`  | Start the MCP server              |
| `stream` | Stream the MCP server over HTTP   |
| `tools`  | Export tools as JSON              |
| `vscode` | Manage VSCode MCP servers         |

## Inherited Flags

| Flag                  | Description                                                  |
| --------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------ |
| `-c, --config string` | path to config file (default: ~/.config/circleci/config.yml) |
| `--debug`             | enable debug logging                                         |
| `-q, --quiet`         | suppress informational output; data on stdout is unaffected  |

## Learn More

- Use `circleci <command> <subcommand> --help` for more information about a command.
- Read the manual at <https://circleci.com/docs/local-cli>
- Support at <https://github.com/CircleCI-Public/circleci-cli/issues>
